---
title: "Project 3"
author: "Lu Bai"
date: "2026-04-18"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
# Load libraries
library(survival)
library(tidyverse)
library(tableone)
library(flextable)
library(survminer) # For KM curve
library(MASS) # For stepAIC (Backward Selection)
library(broom)
```

# Data cleaning and management
```{r}
# Load data
framingham <- read.csv("~/Downloads/frmgham2.csv")

# Data cleaning and management
framingham1 <- framingham %>% filter(PERIOD == 1,
                                     PREVSTRK == 0)

fram_10yr <- framingham1 %>%
  mutate(
    # Time to event is the minimum of stroke date, death date, or 10 years
    # Note: If stroke occurs before death, it is an event.
    fotime = pmin(TIMESTRK, TIMEDTH, 3652, na.rm = TRUE),
    strk_event = if_else(TIMESTRK <= 3652 & STROKE == 1, 1, 0),
    dth_event = if_else(TIMEDTH <= 3652 & STROKE == 0 & DEATH == 1, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(SEX, AGE, DIABETES, SYSBP)
#, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, TOTCHOL, BMI
```

# Generate table 1
```{r}
# Define variables for the table
vars <- c("strk_event", "dth_event", "AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI", "DIABETES", "PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE")
factorVars <- c("strk_event", "dth_event", "DIABETES", "PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE")

# Create the summary stratified by Sex
tab1_obj <- CreateTableOne(vars = vars, 
                           strata = "SEX", 
                           data = fram_10yr, 
                           factorVars = factorVars,
                           test = TRUE,
                           addOverall = TRUE)

# Generate table 1 using flextable
tab1_df <- print(tab1_obj, 
                 missing = TRUE,
                 #quote = FALSE, 
                 noSpaces = TRUE, 
                 printToggle = FALSE,
                 showAllLevels = FALSE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Characteristic") %>%
  select(-c(test))

# Rename rows and columns of table 1
tab1_df$Characteristic <- c("N", "Stroke event, n(%)", "Death, n(%)",
                            "Age, mean(SD)", "Systolic blood pressure, mean(SD)",
                            "Total cholesterol, mean(SD)", "BMI, mean(SD)", 
                            "Diabetic, n(%)","Prevalent coronary heart disease, n(%)",
                            "Use of anti-hypertensive meds, n(%)", "Smoking, n(%)")
colnames(tab1_df) <- c("Characteristic", "Overall", 
                      "Men", "Women", "p-value",
                      "Rate of Missing (%)")

ft1 <- flextable(tab1_df) %>%
  set_header_labels(Characteristic = "Baseline Characteristic") %>%
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  align(j = 1:6, align = "center", part = "body") %>%
  #italic(i = ~ str_detect(Characteristic, "mean|%"), j = 1) %>%
  autofit() %>%
  add_footer_lines("Note: Stroke event indicates participants had the first occurrence of stroke within the 10-year follow-up; Censoring in stroke event includes death without stroke and completion of 10-year follow-up; Death indicates participants die within the 10-year follow-up.") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  set_caption("Table 1. Descriptive statistics of baseline characteristics, the number of stroke and death event by sex")

# Display table
ft1
```

# Kaplan-Meier Curves
```{r}
# Fit KM Curve for Secondary risk factors
km_chd <- survfit(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ PREVCHD, data = fram_10yr)
km_bpmeds <- survfit(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ BPMEDS, data = fram_10yr)
km_smoke <- survfit(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ CURSMOKE, data = fram_10yr)

# Generate individual plots
plot_chd <- ggsurvplot(km_chd, data = fram_10yr, break.time.by = 365,
                       xlim = c(0, 3652.5), ylim = c(0.8, 1), 
                       title = "Prevalent CHD", font.title = c(12),
                       legend.labs = c("No", "Yes"), 
                       xlab = "Days of Follow-up", 
                       ylab = "Stroke-Free Survival Probability",
                       ggtheme = theme_minimal())

plot_bpmeds <- ggsurvplot(km_bpmeds, data = fram_10yr, break.time.by = 365,
                          xlim = c(0, 3652.5), ylim = c(0.8, 1),
                          title = "Use of Anti-hypertensive Meds", font.title = c(12),
                          xlab = "Days of Follow-up",
                          ylab = "Stroke-Free Survival Probability",
                          legend.labs = c("No", "Yes"), ggtheme = theme_minimal())

plot_smoke <- ggsurvplot(km_smoke, data = fram_10yr, break.time.by = 365,
                         xlim = c(0, 3652.5), ylim = c(0.8, 1),
                         title = "Current Smoking", font.title = c(12),
                         xlab = "Days of Follow-up",
                         ylab = "Stroke-Free Survival Probability",
                         legend.labs = c("Non-Smoker", "Smoker"), ggtheme = theme_minimal())

# Arrange in two columns
splots <- list(plot_chd, plot_bpmeds, plot_smoke)
arrange_ggsurvplots(splots, print = TRUE, ncol = 2, nrow = 2,
                    title = "Figure 1. 10-Year Stroke-Free Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Secondary Risk Factors",
                    font.main = c(18, "bold"))
```

# Secondary risk factor backward selection
```{r}
# Model selection (Backward)
# Fit full model with all primary and secondary factors for Males
# Define all variables involved in the selection process
vars_to_keep <- c("AGE", "DIABETES", "SYSBP", "PREVCHD", 
                  "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "TOTCHOL", "BMI", 
                  "fotime", "strk_event")

# Create a clean subset for the female-stratified analysis
dat_complete <- fram_10yr %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(vars_to_keep)) %>%
  drop_na() 

mod_full <- coxph(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP + PREVCHD + BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI, data = dat_complete)

# Perform backward selection based on AIC
mod_final <- step(mod_full, direction = "backward")
```

```{r}
# Manually create the data frame based on stepwise output
aic_summary <- data.frame(
  Step = c("Start", "Step 1", "Step 2", "Step 3", "Step 4 (Final)"),
  Model_Formula = c(
    "Full Model (All Variables)",
    "- TOTCHOL",
    "- BMI",
    "- PREVCHD",
    "- BPMEDS"
  ),
  AIC = c(1611.4, 1609.56, 1607.79, 1606.62, 1606.52),
  Variables_Remaining = c(
    "All", 
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, BMI, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, Smoke",
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, Smoke",
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, BPMEDS, Smoke",
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, Smoke"
  )
)

# Generate a summary table
aic_table <- flextable(aic_summary) %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Step = "Iteration",
    Model_Formula = "Variable Removed",
    AIC = "AIC Value",
    Variables_Remaining = "Predictors"
  ) %>%
  bold(i = 5, part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = 5, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>%
  set_caption("Table 2. Summary of Backward Model Selection via AIC") %>%
  autofit() %>%
  theme_booktabs()

# Display the table
aic_table
```

# Fit final model

```{r}
# Fit final model
mod_male <- coxph(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP + CURSMOKE, data = filter(fram_10yr, SEX == 1))

mod_female <- coxph(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP + CURSMOKE, data = filter(fram_10yr, SEX == 2))

# Extract and clean the results for both models
# calculate Hazard Ratios (exp(coef)) and 95% Confidence Intervals
tidy_male <- tidy(mod_male, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(Model = "Male Cox PH Model")

tidy_female <- tidy(mod_female, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(Model = "Female Cox PH Model")

# Combine the data
summary_df <- bind_rows(tidy_male, tidy_female) %>%
  dplyr::select(Model, term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~round(., 3)),
    p.value = if_else(p.value < 0.001, "< 0.001", as.character(round(p.value, 3))),
    HR_CI = paste0(estimate, " (", conf.low, "-", conf.high, ")")
  )

# Create the Flextable
tab_mod_summary <- summary_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Model, term, HR_CI, p.value) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  merge_v(j = "Model") %>%
  set_header_labels(
    term = "Risk Factor",
    HR_CI = "Hazard Ratio (95% CI)",
    p.value = "P-Value"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Table 2: Adjusted Hazard Ratios for 10-Year Stroke Risk by Sex") %>%
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(gsub("< ", "", p.value)) < 0.05, j = "p.value") %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  valign(j = "Model", valign = "top") %>%
  add_footer_lines("Note: Models adjusted for Age, Diabetes, Systolic BP, and Current Smoking status based on backward AIC selection.") %>%
  autofit()

tab_mod_summary
```
\pagebreak

## Code Appendix

```{r ref.label=knitr::all_labels(appendix == TRUE), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE, include=TRUE}
```
